title: fix: snapshot engine loads entire table into memory — OOM on large tables
description: snapshot_table() calls client.query() which buffers all rows into Vec<Row> before returning. A 10M-row table at 500 bytes/row = 5GB in process memory before a single event is emitted. config.batch_size is only used as a checkpoint interval counter, not to limit fetch size. Fix with a server-side cursor: DECLARE snap_cursor CURSOR FOR SELECT...; loop { FETCH batch_size FROM snap_cursor }. Also: batch_size = 0 panics at mod-zero at L459 — add validation in config.
close_reason: Fixed: server-side cursor via DECLARE/FETCH replaces client.query() which buffered all rows; uses FETCH FORWARD batch_size FROM snap_cursor in a loop. Added batch_size>0 validation in SnapshotConfig::validate().

title: fix: snapshot transaction lifetime — export_snapshot commits before snapshot_table can use it
description: export_snapshot() opens BEGIN, calls pg_export_snapshot(), then COMMITS. PostgreSQL docs: snapshot is only valid until the exporting transaction ends. snapshot_table() opens a new BEGIN and calls SET TRANSACTION SNAPSHOT with a now-dead snapshot_id — always fails with ERROR: invalid snapshot identifier. Snapshot engine emits zero rows in production. Fix requires a second non-replication connection to hold the export transaction open for the duration of the entire snapshot run, only committing after all tables are scanned. Also: pg_conn uses options='--replication=database' which forbids ordinary SQL — detect_pk_columns catalog queries and SELECT * scans need a separate regular connection regardless.
close_reason: Fixed: keeper connection now holds pg_export_snapshot() transaction open for entire snapshot run; worker connection uses SET TRANSACTION SNAPSHOT per table. Added connect_plain() to connection.rs for non-replication connections.

title: fix: missing ROLLBACK on error paths in snapshot_table and export_snapshot
description: After BEGIN in snapshot_table (L369), every ? exit (detect_pk_columns L377, row_to_json L418, channel send L452) returns Err without issuing ROLLBACK. Connection is left in aborted transaction state; next query fails with ERROR: current transaction is aborted. Same issue in export_snapshot (L265-277) if LSN query or parse fails. Fix: wrap transaction body so any error triggers client.batch_execute(ROLLBACK).await.ok() before propagating. Consider a RAII transaction guard.
close_reason: Fixed: worker table transactions use tokio_postgres::Transaction which auto-rollbacks on Drop; keeper transaction in run() wraps run_inner() with ROLLBACK on any error via match statement.

title: Audit and replace ~35 .unwrap() calls in decoder
description: Decoder has ~35 .unwrap() calls — highest density in codebase. Any malformed WAL byte in replication stream panics and crashes entire CDC pipeline. Codebase has been systematically removing unwraps since P2. Fix: protocol field unwraps → .ok_or(TapError::Decode(...)). Schema cache lookups → .ok_or_else(...). Critical for wal2json labeled experimental.
close_reason: Audit complete: all 35 .unwrap() calls are in test code only. Zero production unwraps. No changes needed.

title: Non-unique event IDs within a transaction
description: All events in a multi-DML transaction get identical IDs (format!("{}:{}", lsn, tx_id) in L381's loop). Downstream consumers using ID for deduplication silently drop events 2–N. Fix: add per-event sequence counter: format!("{}:{}:{}", lsn, tx_id, idx).
close_reason: Fix applied: event IDs now include per-transaction index via .enumerate(), format changed from "{lsn}:{tx_id}" to "{lsn}:{tx_id}:{idx}"

title: Silent data loss on nested Begin — pending_events.clear()
description: decode_begin() unconditionally calls self.pending_events.clear(), silently discarding all DML from a prior uncommitted transaction. No error, no log, no recovery. Triggered by duplicate Begin before Commit (stream reconnection edge case). Fix: warn+flush pending events before clearing, or return error. Never silently drop CDC data.
close_reason: Fix applied: pending_events.clear() now guarded with tracing::warn! when discarding uncommitted events on nested Begin

title: Negative count → OOM/process abort on malformed WAL
description: Signed-to-usize cast of read_i16/read_i32 produces usize::MAX when protocol bytes are 0xFFFF (−1). Triggers Vec::with_capacity(usize::MAX) → process abort. Affects L415 (Relation column count), L468 (Relation count), L497 (tuple column count). Fix: validate ≥0 before cast, or use read_u16/read_u32.
close_reason: Fix applied: three sites (decode_relation ncols, skip_truncate nrels, parse_tuple_data ncols) now validate positive before as usize cast, returning TapError::Decode on negative values

title: tap-fmt-fix: Fix cargo fmt failure on builder.rs L180
description: CI format check fails: assert_eq! line in test_builder_all_ops (builder.rs:180) exceeds max line width. Run cargo fmt --all to fix. Simple fix, should be done before merging PR #2.
close_reason: Closed

title: v0.1.0 Foundation — Tap CDC Platform
description: First release of Tap CDC platform: Postgres logical replication → ChangeEvent envelope → HTTP/2 SSE → TypeScript SDK. 11 phases across Rust core, CLI, TS SDK, and CI. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md for full details.

title: fix: std::sync::Mutex held across synchronous SQLite I/O in async context
description: write_snapshot_progress and other StateStore calls use std::sync::Mutex (L461, L479, L530, L540, L547) while performing SQLite I/O. This blocks the Tokio worker thread, starving other async tasks. Also: .lock().unwrap() on all 5 sites panics if mutex is ever poisoned, causing cascade failure. Fix: replace unwrap with map_err to TapError::Snapshot. For blocking I/O: move SQLite calls into tokio::task::spawn_blocking or switch Arc<Mutex<StateStore>> to Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<StateStore>>.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced std::sync::Mutex with tokio::sync::Mutex for StateStore; all .lock().unwrap() calls replaced with .lock().await (tokio::sync::Mutex yields instead of blocking worker thread).

title: fix: SQL injection via unquoted table.qualified from SnapshotConfig::tables
description: table.qualified from SnapshotConfig::tables is interpolated directly into SQL in snapshot_table (L396-399). SourceConfig::tables goes through validate_identifier() but SnapshotConfig::tables does not — TapConfig::validate() only calls self.source.validate(). Config entry like tables = ["public.users; DROP TABLE users; --"] executes injection. Fix: (1) add validate_identifier loop over snapshot.tables in TapConfig::validate(), (2) quote identifiers with double-quotes in format string using schema/name split, (3) also quote pk_columns in order_clause.
close_reason: Fixed: all SQL identifiers now quoted via quote_ident() helper (escapes embedded double-quotes); qualified_sql() builds '"schema"."table"' for FROM/JOIN clauses; pk_columns quoted in ORDER BY; added snapshot.tables validation in TapConfig::validate().

title: Fix unwrap_or_default() silently swallowing corrupt schema JSON
description:
## Problem
store.rs:362 — `serde_json::from_str(&pks_str).unwrap_or_default()` silently returns `Vec::new()` if primary_keys JSON is corrupt instead of surfacing the error.

## Impact
Caller receives empty primary key list with no way to know data is corrupt. Could cascade into wrong deduplication/upsert logic in CDC pipeline.

## Fix
Match the error-handling pattern at L335-337: propagate as `TapError::StateCorruption`.

## PR Context
Found in PR #5 review
close_reason: Fixed in PR #5: backup now checks path before Connection::open; schema JSON parse propagates StateCorruption; snapshot UPSERT preserves completed status

title: Fix backup overwrite on every open
description:
## Problem
store.rs:139-144 — `Connection::open(path)` at L117 auto-creates the file, so `path.exists()` at L139 is always true. Backup runs on every `open()`, not once per session as the doc comment claims (L98-99).

## Impact
If the DB corrupts mid-session, the next `open()` overwrites `state.db.bak` with corrupt data — destroying the only recovery path.

## Fix
Move `path.exists()` check to BEFORE `Connection::open()`.
Also correct doc comment at L98-99.

## PR Context
Found in PR #5 review
close_reason: Fixed in PR #5: backup now checks path before Connection::open; schema JSON parse propagates StateCorruption; snapshot UPSERT preserves completed status

title: Fix write_snapshot_progress unconditionally resetting completed status
description:
## Problem
store.rs L268: UPSERT clause sets `status = 'in_progress'` in both INSERT and ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE paths. If a snapshot was already `completed` and `write_snapshot_progress` is called again (retry, worker restart), status is silently reverted.

## Impact
Sequence: in_progress → complete_snapshot → completed → write_snapshot_progress → in_progress ❌

## Fix
On conflict, only update `rows_count`, preserve existing status:
```sql
ON CONFLICT(table_name, snapshot_id) DO UPDATE SET rows_count = ?3
```
Or add explicit `restart_snapshot()` method and guard the upsert.

## PR Context
Found in PR #5 review (https://github.com/levu304/tap/pull/5#discussion_r3331771920)
close_reason: Fixed in PR #5: backup now checks path before Connection::open; schema JSON parse propagates StateCorruption; snapshot UPSERT preserves completed status

title: WalDecoder trait missing flush() — events trapped on disconnect
description: No flush/finalize method on WalDecoder trait. If stream disconnects after Begin+DML but before Commit, pending_events are trapped forever. Since decoder persists across reconnections, this is both memory leak and data loss. Fix: add fn flush(&mut self) -> Vec<ChangeEvent> to trait, call on disconnect.
close_reason: Fix applied: flus() default method added to WalDecoder trait; PgoutputDecoder implementation drains pending_events into :flush-suffixed ChangeEvents

title: Commit without Begin → malformed events with empty tx_id
description: unwrap_or_default() on Option<String> produces "" when Commit arrives without Begin. Events get IDs like "0/ABCDEF:" and tx_id = "". Consumers validating tx_id may crash. Fix: fall back to "UNKNOWN" sentinel + log warning.
close_reason: Fix applied: Commit without Begin now returns TapError::Decode error instead of producing empty tx_id

title: SourceMetadata.db always empty — missing database attribution
description: db: String::new() hardcodes database field to empty for all pgoutput events. Multi-database CDC deployments cannot route/filter by source. Fix: accept db_name in PgoutputDecoder::new() or add set_database().
close_reason: Fix applied: PgoutputDecoder and Wal2JsonDecoder now accept db_name parameter; create_decoder signature updated; SourceMetadata.db populated from decoder's db_name field

title: Unbounded pending_events — OOM on large transactions
description: Events accumulate in Vec between Begin and Commit with no upper bound. A single bulk-insert transaction with millions of rows exhausts memory. Fix: add MAX_EVENTS_PER_TXN = 10,000 and return error on overflow.
close_reason: Fix applied: MAX_EVENTS_PER_TXN=10000 constant added with bounds check before push in decode_insert/decode_update/decode_delete

title: tap-config-validate: Config structs accept invalid/empty required fields silently
description: SourceConfig with #[serde(default)] silently accepts empty dbname, user, password — Postgres connection fails with obscure error. TOML parse error loses line/column info (config.rs:80-82 — use toml::de::Error::line_col()). ReconnectConfig has all pub fields, no construction validation (max_retries=0, max_backoff < initial, zero initial backoff all accepted). Fix: remove struct-level #[serde(default)], apply only to optional fields. Add validate() to each config struct. Add ReconnectConfig::new() with validation.
close_reason: Closed

title: tap-debug-password: Password field leaks via Debug derive on SourceConfig/TapConfig
description: Security: #[derive(Debug)] on TapConfig (config.rs:55) and SourceConfig (config.rs:88) transitively exposes password: String in debug/log output. Any dbg!(&config), tracing::debug! or panic message prints plaintext Postgres replication password to logs. Fix: implement manual Debug for SourceConfig with <REDACTED> for password field. Consider secrecy::Secret<String> for compile-time protection.
close_reason: Closed

title: tap-monotonic-ts: SystemTime::now() produces non-monotonic timestamps
description: Critical bug in ChangeEventBuilder::build() (builder.rs:92-95): SystemTime::now() can jump backward during NTP corrections, leap seconds, or VM clock drift. Downstream consumers relying on ts_ms for event ordering will see incorrect ordering. unwrap_or(0) silently masks pre-epoch clocks (returns 0 for all events). Fix: use Instant + atomic counter for guaranteed monotonicity, or at minimum add a monotonic guard that ensures ts_ms never decreases.
close_reason: Closed

title: P10: CI & Testing — 40+ unit, 8 integration, E2E, cargo-deny/audit
description: Implement test infrastructure: 40+ Rust unit tests across core types/postgres/WAL/state/snapshot/SSE modules. 8 integration tests using testcontainers: full snapshot+streaming, schema changes, reconnection, crash recovery, multi-table capture, empty database, resume from checkpoint, wal2json, SSE client consumption. E2E smoke test (bash): init→capture→verify events. CI: test (ubuntu, postgres:14/15/16 service) + test-unit (ubuntu+macOS, unit only) + audit (cargo deny + cargo audit). ~5 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P10.

title: P9: SDK (napi-rs) — Tap class, ThreadsafeFunction bridge, dual delivery
description: Implement napi-rs binding: Tap class with constructor(config: TapConfig), on('change'/'error'/'status') handlers, start()→Promise<string>, stop()→Promise<void>, pause()/resume()→Promise<void>. ThreadsafeFunction bridge: async callback from tokio→Node event loop, dual channel (unbounded for SSE, bounded for callbacks), backpressure propagation (pause WAL when full). Typed interfaces for TS consumers. 5+6 platform-specific npm packages. ~7 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P9.

title: P8: CLI — 5 clap commands with config and orchestration
description: Implement CLI binary with clap derive: 4 subcommands (init/capture/inspect/dev) + test placeholder. tap init: scaffold project, create slot/publication, generate TS types. tap capture: load config, check checkpoint, snapshot→streaming, start SSE server, live status output. tap inspect: introspect schema, output TS/JSON. tap dev: capture + minimal status page. Global flags: --config, --log-level, --log-format. ~8 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P8.

title: P7: SSE Server — axum HTTP/2 with 8 event types + health/status
description: Implement SseServer with axum HTTP/2: 8 SSE event types (change/heartbeat/snapshot_start/snapshot_progress/snapshot_complete/streaming_start/error/shutdown). Broadcast channel (tokio::sync::broadcast). GET /events - SSE stream, GET /health - 200/503, GET /status - CaptureStatus JSON. Last-Event-ID resume to nearest commit boundary. Heartbeat every 10s idle. Configurable buffer via max_buffer_size (10k default). ~3 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P7.

title: P6: Snapshot Engine — sequential scan with pg_export_snapshot()
description: Implement SnapshotRunner: BEGIN READ ONLY + pg_export_snapshot(), ordered SELECT * FROM table ORDER BY PK, emit op:'r' ChangeEvent per row, checkpoint every 10,000 rows via snapshots table. Handle: empty tables (no-op), no-PK tables (ctid checkpoint with warning), composite PKs (key=value:key=value format). Transition: save snapshot export LSN, switch to streaming. ~3 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P6.
close_reason: P6 Snapshot Engine implemented by rust-engineer: SnapshotRunner with pg_export_snapshot(), sequential table scan, checkpoint every 10k rows. Handles empty/no-PK/composite PK tables, resume from interrupt, table discovery from publication. 131 tests passing, 0 clippy warnings. Branch: current

title: P5: SQLite State Store — checkpoint/offsets/snapshots
description: Implement StateStore with rusqlite: 6 tables (offsets, snapshots, schema_cache, events, skipped_lsns, migrations). PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL. Batch checkpointing: flush every 1000 events or 1000ms. Backup: copy on open. Migration: schema_version table with up/down. Recovery: integrity_check on corrupt, restore from .bak. ~3 files. Can be parallel with P3-P4. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P5.
close_reason: P5 SQLite State Store implemented: StateStore with 6 tables, WAL mode, batch checkpointing, backup/restore, migration framework. 22 state-specific tests. All 114 tests passing.

title: P4: WAL Decoding — pgoutput (first-class) + wal2json (experimental)
description: Implement WalDecoder trait (decode(&[u8])→Vec<WalEvent>). PgoutputDecoder: hand-written parser for pgoutput binary protocol (Begin/Insert/Update/Delete/Commit/Relation/Truncate messages) with schema cache HashMap<RelationId, RelationSchema>. Key: streaming state machine over CopyBothBytes. Wal2JsonDecoder: EXPERIMENTAL, parses JSON-per-transaction format. ~2 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P4.
close_reason: P4 WAL Decoding implemented — pgoutput (hand-written binary parser, 25 tests) + wal2json (experimental). 85/85 tests passing, 0 clippy warnings. Branch: feature/tap-36n-wal-decoding

title: P3: Postgres Connection — replication slot lifecycle
description: Implement PgConnection wrapping tokio-postgres replication client: connection with replication=database mode, Lsn newtype with ordering/display/serialization, create_replication_slot (name validation), create_publication (FOR ALL TABLES / FOR TABLE), drop logic, validate_permissions, slot_exists/pub_exists checks. Key: PgConnection lifecycle model (Disconnected→Connecting→Ready→Streaming→Draining→Disconnected). ~3 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P3.
close_reason: Closed

title: P2: Core Types — domain models and errors
description: Implement ChangeEvent struct, SourceMetadata, ChangeEventBuilder with serde Serialize/Deserialize, TapConfig deserialized from TOML, TapError enum via thiserror (connection/replication/decode/snapshot/state/io/sse variants), ReconnectConfig. Key test: ChangeEvent round-trips through JSON, builder handles all op types. ~8 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P2.
close_reason: P2: Core Types implemented — ChangeEvent, SourceMetadata, Operation, ChangeEventBuilder, TapConfig, TapError, ReconnectConfig. 30 tests passing. All verifications green.

title: P1: Scaffold — monorepo, toolchain, skeleton
description: Set up moon monorepo structure, Cargo workspace (rust-version 1.85, edition 2024), pnpm workspace, prototools. Create crates/tap-core library crate, crates/tap-cli binary crate, packages/sdk-ts napi-rs skeleton, .github/workflows/ci.yml stub, .gitignore. Verify: moon run build succeeds, cargo check passes, pnpm install works. ~20 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P1 for details.
close_reason: Phase 1 scaffold complete: moon monorepo, Cargo workspace, 3 crates building, CI workflow, all verifications pass

title: Fix: validate_tables() parameter mismatch — doesn't match ensure_publication config tables
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: row.get(1) type assumption — pg_lsn→String mapping may panic at runtime
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: Lsn.0 is pub — broken newtype encapsulation, bypasses FromStr validation
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: Password leakage via TapError::PostgresConnection error propagation
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: No TLS — cleartext credentials and WAL data over tokio_postgres::NoTls
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: Dropped JoinHandle — silent background connection failures & swallowed panics
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: SQL injection in ensure_publication + config validation gap for publication/tables/plugin
close_reason: Closed

title: tap-test-gaps: Missing test coverage for error paths and edge cases
description: 30 tests have good happy-path coverage but gaps in: (1) build() with zero setters (bare ChangeEventBuilder::new().build()), (2) TapConfig::from_path with valid file (only tests nonexistent path), (3) Config serde round-trip (serialize→deserialize), (4) Operation::from_str("") empty string, (5) from_path with malformed TOML, (6) SourceMetadata snapshot: Some(false) behavior, (7) config parsing with unknown TOML fields, (8) ReconnectConfig::default() values, (9) build() with before(None)+after(None), (10) malformed JSON deserialization. ~7 critical tests worth adding now. See PR #2 review for details.
close_reason: Closed

title: tap-builder-validate: ChangeEventBuilder::build() silently defaults missing fields
description: builder.rs:91-113: build() returns ChangeEvent with no Result — silently defaults missing op to Read and missing source to empty SourceMetadata. Forgotten .op()/.source() calls produce semantically wrong events. If validation is ever added to build(), this path panics before tests catch it. Fix: change build() -> Result<ChangeEvent, TapError>. Require op and source to be set. Remove implicit Read default.
close_reason: Closed

title: tap-type-hardening: Type safety gaps — op: String, LSN newtype, error categorization
description: Several type design issues to harden before more code builds on weak foundations: (1) ChangeEvent.op: String accepts any string despite Operation enum existing — use custom serde through Operation::from_str (envelope.rs:42). (2) lsn/tx_id as raw Strings with no format validation — define Lsn newtype with FromStr, Ord, Display (envelope.rs:70). (3) Operation::from_str returns TapError::Config for invalid codes — should be decode/replication error, not config file error (envelope.rs:142-144). (4) Implement std::str::FromStr for Operation (clippy). (5) Other(String) prints untrusted input verbatim (error/mod.rs:53-54). (6) Stringly-typed LogFormat/LogLevel config fields (config.rs:248-250). Also: move impl Default for SourceMetadata from builder.rs to envelope.rs.
close_reason: Closed

title: P11: Release — binaries, npm publishing, quickstart
description: Ship v0.1.0: build 5 platform targets (darwin-arm64, darwin-x64, linux-x64-gnu, linux-arm64-gnu, linux-x64-musl). Scripted release process (scripts/release.sh). npm publishing of @tap/sdk with platform-specific packages via @napi-rs/cli. README with quickstart: install→tap init→tap capture→npm run consume. No macOS code signing for v0.1.0. ~8 files. See .docs/plans/v0.1.0-implementation-plan.md P11.
